codeReview.summary: The commit makes the mission service manifest usable on this machine, but it does not actually satisfy the feature's runtime-baseline requirements because it rewrites the mission backend contract from 'reuse the existing backend on 8001' to 'start and stop a worker-managed backend', and the new stop command can terminate any healthy backend already running on 8001.

issues.description: The new backend `start` command no longer follows the mission boundary to reuse the existing backend on port 8001. If any process is already healthy on 8001, the manifest exits early with `backend-already-healthy-on-8001` and does not record ownership, but the paired `stop` command will still kill whatever PID is stored in `.logs/mission-backend-8001.pid` from an earlier run. This changes the contract from non-invasive reuse to process ownership and makes later workers able to kill an unrelated shared mission backend.

The backend `stop` command unconditionally kills the PID in `.logs/mission-backend-8001.pid` without verifying that the process is the worker-started Uvicorn instance for this repo or that port 8001 still belongs to that process. Because the feature and AGENTS guidance require reusing the existing backend instead of stopping/replacing it, this stop behavior can take down the shared mission runtime and violate the stated environment boundary.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill says runtime features should verify contracts with curl on port 8001, but it does not explicitly reconcile that with the mission rule to reuse an already-running backend instead of adding process-management logic. The worker used that ambiguity to change `.factory/services.yaml` and take ownership of backend startup/stop.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md instructs workers to verify local backend behavior, while mission AGENTS.md says 'Reuse the existing backend process already serving 8001; do not stop or replace it'. The handoff's skillFeedback also notes this deviation and asks for clearer guidance.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission service manifest now encodes worker-managed backend lifecycle on port 8001, which conflicts with the documented environment/library guidance that 8001 is a shared reused backend. Shared state docs should make the service manifest match that boundary explicitly so future workers do not alternate between reusing and owning the same port.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml backend.start/stop now launch and kill Uvicorn locally, while .factory/missions/f5a15d60-e8ab-48fc-95a4-044b49b5770b/AGENTS.md and .factory/library/environment.md both say to reuse the existing backend on 8001 and not stop or replace it.

summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, worker handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, router mounts, and shared-state docs. Result: fail, because the only code change was to `.factory/services.yaml`, and that change violates the mission's explicit shared-backend boundary by introducing worker-owned backend start/stop behavior that can terminate the shared service on port 8001.
